fields.description:
## Why
Rec 6 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md. The JSON Schema system (SSOT schemas, $ref-shared common.schema.json, coverage guard test_schema_coverage.py) is a strength — keep it. But three contracts agents depend on are unfrozen, and the breaking-change window after the 2026-06-09 internal announcement is the cheap time to freeze them: a 500-person rollout soon means mixed rebar versions sharing one origin/tickets.

## What
(a) Exit-code contract: 0/1/2/10 are load-bearing for agents but scattered through script headers — author one canonical doc (docs/exit-codes.md or a section in output-schemas.md) + a conformance test.
(b) Error envelope: extend the error_envelope schema to every command's failure mode so agents never parse stderr prose; error-shape changes are breaking — decide now.
(c) Event-schema versioning: docs/event-schema.md is the true wire format BETWEEN CLONES of different rebar versions. Declare it formally versioned; add + test a reducer rule that unknown event TYPEs are preserved-and-ignored (forward compat).
(d) Close the mcp_server.py pydantic-model-vs-JSON-schema equivalence pin fully (test_schema_outputs.py is partial).

## Scope
Contracts and docs + their conformance tests; no behavioral redesign. Anything that changes an emitted error shape lands inside the window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical exit-code contract documented + conformance-tested
- [ ] error_envelope covers every command failure mode; documented as the only machine-readable error channel
- [ ] Event schema carries an explicit version + tested unknown-TYPE forward-compat rule
- [ ] MCP outputSchemas pinned equivalent to the JSON Schemas in CI
